text: The camera is introduced into the abdomen, revealing normal intestinal contents. The anterior abdominal wall shows scarring from previous surgery, with the omentum suspended. The surgeon begins to navigate through the scar tissue.

The surgeon uses fine scissors and occasional cautery to dissect through adhesions, freeing the anterior abdominal wall. The robotic system provides high-definition, three-dimensional imagery, allowing precise manipulation of tissue despite the lack of tactile feedback.

The surgeon releases adhesions using cautery, carefully navigating through fat tissue. As the dissection approaches the intestines, the surgeon proceeds with caution to avoid injury, highlighting the importance of slow and deliberate movements.
